annotations.label: Bell's theorem (statement)

anchor.snippet: Bell's theorem is a term encompassing a number of closely related results in physics

annotations.note: The general Bell's theorem is not formalized, but Mathlib's CHSH inequality (and Tsirelson) in `Mathlib/Algebra/Star/CHSH.lean` provides the algebraic core of one of its main instances.

annotations.label: Locality (in Bell's theorem)

anchor.snippet: "local" refers to the principle of locality

annotations.note: No physics-level definition of locality / spacelike separation exists in Mathlib (CHSH only encodes it via the commutation hypothesis in `IsCHSHTuple`).

annotations.label: Hidden variables

anchor.snippet: " Hidden variables " are supposed properties of quantum particles

annotations.note: Mathlib has no formal notion of a hidden-variable theory.

annotations.label: Bell inequality

anchor.snippet: Such a constraint would later be named a Bell inequality

annotations.note: Bell inequalities in general are not defined, but the canonical CHSH Bell inequality is proved as `CHSH_inequality_of_comm`.

annotations.label: CHSH inequality derivation

anchor.snippet: This is a Bell inequality, specifically, the CHSH inequality

annotations.note: Proved as `CHSH_inequality_of_comm` for commutative ordered *-algebras over ℝ, which generalizes the classical probability statement.

annotations.label: Assumption of realism

anchor.snippet: sometimes called the assumption of realism

annotations.note: No formal definition of the realism assumption is present in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Assumption of locality

anchor.snippet: often called the assumption of locality

annotations.note: No standalone definition of the locality assumption; only encoded implicitly as commutativity in CHSH tuples.

annotations.label: Quantum violation of CHSH via Bell state

anchor.snippet: Quantum mechanics can violate the CHSH inequality, as follows

annotations.note: Mathlib's CHSH file notes the existence of a 4×4 matrix CHSH tuple attaining 2√2 only as future work; no Bell-state construction is given.

annotations.label: Bell state

anchor.snippet: Victor prepares a pair of qubits which he describes by the Bell state

annotations.note: Mathlib does not formalize the Bell state |Φ+⟩ = (|00⟩+|11⟩)/√2 as a named quantum state.

annotations.label: Pauli matrices

anchor.snippet: the eigenstates of one of the Pauli matrices

annotations.note: Mathlib does not have a dedicated definition of the Pauli matrices σ_x, σ_y, σ_z as named declarations.

annotations.label: Born rule

anchor.snippet: Victor can calculate the quantum expectation values for pairs of these observables using the Born rule

annotations.note: Mathlib has no formalization of the Born rule for quantum measurement probabilities.

annotations.label: Tsirelson bound

anchor.snippet: the largest that quantum physics permits for this combination of expectation values, making it a Tsirelson bound

annotations.note: Tsirelson's bound `A₀B₀ + A₀B₁ + A₁B₀ - A₁B₁ ≤ √2^3 • 1` is proved in `tsirelson_inequality` for general ordered *-algebras over ℝ.

annotations.label: CHSH game

anchor.snippet: The CHSH inequality can also be thought of as a game in which Alice and Bob try to coordinate their actions

annotations.note: The CHSH game / nonlocal-game reformulation is not in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Bell (1964) theorem

anchor.snippet: Bell proves by example that these special cases can be explained in terms of hidden variables, then proceeds to show that the full range of possibilities involving intermediate angles cannot

annotations.note: The original Bell 1964 inequality and its proof are not formalized; only CHSH appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Spin singlet state

anchor.snippet: described by a spin singlet state (which is an example of an entangled state)

annotations.note: The spin singlet state (|↑↓⟩−|↓↑⟩)/√2 is not formalized as a named declaration in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Local hidden-variable model (Bell 1964)

anchor.snippet: Bell posited that a local hidden-variable model for these correlations would explain them in terms of an integral over the possible values of some hidden parameter

annotations.note: There is no formal definition of a local hidden-variable model in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Quantum violation with orthogonal vectors at 45°

anchor.snippet: For example, let the vectors

annotations.note: No formalized worked example of Bell-violating spin measurements is present.

annotations.label: EPR criterion of reality

anchor.snippet: The theorem builds upon the "EPR criterion of reality"

annotations.note: The EPR criterion of reality is not formalized.

annotations.label: GHZ–Mermin theorem

anchor.snippet: Daniel Greenberger , Michael A. Horne , and Anton Zeilinger presented a four-particle thought experiment in 1990

annotations.note: Grepping for GHZ/Greenberger/Mermin in Mathlib returns no results.

annotations.label: GHZ contradiction with quantum prediction

anchor.snippet: But this is inconsistent with quantum physics

annotations.note: No GHZ contradiction example is in Mathlib.

annotations.label: GHZ nonlocal game

anchor.snippet: This thought experiment can also be recast as a traditional Bell inequality or, equivalently, as a nonlocal game

annotations.note: The GHZ nonlocal game is not formalized; Mathlib has no nonlocal-game framework.

annotations.label: Kochen–Specker theorem

anchor.snippet: a Kochen–Specker configuration is an "uncolorable set" that demonstrates the inconsistency of assuming a hidden variable

annotations.note: Grep for Kochen/Specker in Mathlib yields no results.

annotations.label: Kochen–Specker configuration

anchor.snippet: A Kochen–Specker configuration is a finite set of vectors made of multiple interlocking bases

annotations.note: Kochen–Specker configurations are not defined in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Free will theorem (Conway–Kochen)

anchor.snippet: This type of argument gained attention when an instance of it was advanced by John Conway and Simon Kochen under the name of the free will theorem

annotations.note: No Conway–Kochen free-will theorem appears in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Werner states

anchor.snippet: Werner states are bipartite quantum states that are invariant under unitaries of symmetric tensor-product form

annotations.note: No definition of Werner states in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Spekkens toy model

anchor.snippet: Robert Spekkens introduced a toy model that starts with the premise of local, discretized degrees of freedom

annotations.note: Grep for Spekkens / toy model returns no results.

annotations.label: Gleason's theorem

anchor.snippet: Gleason's theorem provided an argument that a broad class of hidden-variable theories are incompatible with quantum mechanics

annotations.note: The Mathlib occurrences of 'Gleason' refer to Jonathan Gleason's Haar-measure write-up and Gleason's projective-topological-spaces result, not Andrew Gleason's theorem on quantum measures.

annotations.label: Counterfactual definiteness

anchor.snippet: the assumption often called counterfactual definiteness or "realism"

annotations.note: Counterfactual definiteness is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Einstein–Podolsky–Rosen paradox

anchor.snippet: building upon the Einstein–Podolsky–Rosen paradox

annotations.note: The EPR paradox is a physics thought experiment with no Mathlib formalization.

annotations.label: Quantum entanglement

anchor.snippet: the phenomenon of quantum entanglement

annotations.note: Quantum entanglement of joint states is not defined in Mathlib; tensor products of Hilbert spaces are present, but no notion of entangled vs. separable states.

annotations.label: Bell test

anchor.snippet: More advanced experiments, known collectively as Bell tests

annotations.note: Bell tests are physical experimental protocols; not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Orthonormal basis of a Hilbert space

anchor.snippet: orthonormal bases for a Hilbert space represent measurements

annotations.note: Orthonormal Hilbert bases are formalized as `HilbertBasis` (and `OrthonormalBasis` for the finite-dimensional case in `Mathlib.Analysis.InnerProductSpace.PiL2`).

annotations.label: Spin singlet anti-correlation prediction

anchor.snippet: the quantum-mechanical prediction for the correlation between two detectors with settings

annotations.note: The quantum prediction E(a,b) = −a·b for the singlet state is not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Superdeterminism

anchor.snippet: A (hypothetical) theory where the choice of measurement is necessarily correlated with the system being measured is known as superdeterministic

annotations.note: Superdeterminism is a foundational/physical concept not formalized in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Detection and locality loopholes

anchor.snippet: The most prevalent loopholes in real experiments are the detection and locality loopholes

annotations.note: Experimental loopholes are physics-experimental notions, not formalized in Mathlib.
